stories: Trump proposed 20% toll on Strait of Hormuz cargo July 13, scrapped July 14 under Gulf ally pressure after Brazil and Gulf states called it piracy; US Iran blockade reinstated
June LMI 71.1, fastest expansion since March 2022; transportation prices sub-index 92.4 near-record; capacity 7th consecutive month of contraction
June 2026 container imports 2.4M TEU +8.2% YoY, China imports 814K TEU +27.4% YoY as shippers front-loaded ahead of Section 301 July 24 deadline
Transpacific ocean rates at $7,500/FEU West Coast and $9,000+/FEU East Coast amid Hormuz closure and peak season convergence
C.H. Robinson Q2 earnings scheduled July 29 with consensus $1.49 EPS (+15.5% YoY); 30 AI agents completing 100% of rate quotes in 32 seconds; July 2026 update forecasts +34% YoY spot TL
RXO Q2 2026 EBITDA guided to $27-37M vs Q1 adjusted EBITDA loss; agentic AI processed 500K+ carrier calls in Q1 driving cost-per-load reductions
FreightWaves Supply Chain AI Symposium held July 15 in Chicago featuring Augment, project44, GenLogs, HappyRobot with AI Excellence Award presented live

Blue Yonder named Gartner TMS Magic Quadrant Leader for 19th consecutive time; deployed named persistent-memory agents (warehouse, logistics, inventory ops) on Snowflake/Azure as autonomous execution layer
SAP accelerating Joule agents across TMS, procurement, supply chain for autonomous execution embedded in existing ERP stacks targeting large shippers
DAT July 9: dry van spot $3.05/mile crossed above contract rates for first time since February 2022; flatbed $3.67/mile all-time record; reefer $3.43/mile; all up 39%+ YoY
Post-Independence Day flatbed load-to-truck ratio surged to 53.3 from 42.2; van LTR 12.1; June LMI capacity sub-index contracting 7th consecutive month
CMA CGM agreed July 1 to acquire FedEx Supply Chain for $1.4B enterprise value, tripling CEVA Logistics North American footprint to 240 locations
Section 301 tariffs T-9 days to July 24 implementation covering 60 countries at 10-12.5% with no expiry date replacing SCOTUS-struck IEEPA tariffs
USMCA third bilateral US-Mexico negotiating round scheduled week of July 20; July 1 joint review deadline passed without renewal agreement; Canada excluded
FMCSA finalized three deregulatory rules effective July 22: removes CDL self-reporting, rescinds physical ELD manual, revises inspection corrected-report procedures
Nine transportation and logistics companies filed for bankruptcy in early July 2026 with ~250 jobs eliminated; structural carrier exits outpacing new-authority formation

Craig-Hallum initiated Aurora Innovation at Buy ($18 target) calling it physical AI with $100B+ potential; Gen-2 observerless hardware launching Q3 2026 targeting 200 trucks year-end with Uber Freight as named customer

project44 split into two standalone businesses July 14: project44 (Decision Intelligence for enterprise shippers) and LSP44 (AI-native agent/API infrastructure for 3PLs and brokers), already powering 9 of the world's 10 largest LSPs
LSP44 positions as embedded freight infrastructure layer sharing one AI-native carrier network and data graph with project44 - described as AWS equivalent for freight intermediaries
Saia LTL Freight 7.1% GRI effective July 6 - 120bps above ABF's June 22 +5.9% and 3 months ahead of typical industry cadence, driven by TL overflow freight filling LTL networks
Aurora Innovation Q2 2026 Business Review call scheduled July 29; expected to confirm Gen-2 observerless hardware fleet status and progress toward 200+ driverless trucks year-end
Aurora Gen-2 hardware (International LT Series) confirmed in observerless mode: 1M-mile hardware lifespan, 1,000m FirstLight lidar range, 50% cost-per-truck reduction vs Gen-1

US-Iran ceasefire collapsed July 8 after Iran struck commercial vessels in Strait of Hormuz; CENTCOM reinstated US naval blockade July 14 sending Brent crude +9.59% to $83.30/bbl; re-talks emerging July 15-16
DAT July 9: dry van $2.97/mi, flatbed $3.69/mi all-time record, reefer $3.45/mi - all 40-50% above YoY; spot van crossed above contract rates for first time since February 2022
Post-July 4 load rebound: postings +24.7% WoW, truck postings -2.6%, Market Demand Index +37.1 points; flatbed L-T-T 53.3 from 42.2

Section 301 tariffs T-8 days to July 24: 10-12.5% on 60+ countries (99.4% of US imports by value), zero grandfathering, no extension announced; JOC warns compliance uncertainty freezing Q3 freight decisions
USMCA Round 3 begins week of July 20 in Mexico City; open issues dropped from 54 to 14 with automotive rules of origin, agriculture, and economic security remaining; Canada excluded
Two Chapter 11 carrier filings recorded July 16 (Division 2 Truck Co., ODM Truck) as part of continuing July bankruptcy wave
LSP44 raises platform architecture question for Transplace-based 4PL: whether to integrate with LSP44 carrier API/agent spine or maintain independent infrastructure
LTL GRI wave (Saia +7.1%, ABF +5.9%, ODFL +4.9%) reshaping 4PL mode economics as TL overflow fills LTL networks; optimal mode selection shifting weekly
Uber Technologies announced $14.8B acquisition of Delivery Hero July 16 requiring ~€14B new debt, extending platform to 99 markets raising capital allocation question for Uber Freight
ArcBest announced July 16: ~280 job cuts, 10 LTL service center closures, brand consolidation of MoLo/Panther/ArcBest Technologies under ArcBest effective Aug 1; $40M annualized savings
Envoy AI launched Ellie Workforce July 14: browser-native autonomous freight execution OS for brokers covering carrier sourcing, rate negotiation, compliance, shipment monitoring
Fr8Tech completed transformation from online freight broker to pure-play AI software company with Fleet Rocket agentic platform for USMCA corridor July 15
Diesel surged 21.8 cents/gal WoW to $4.796/gal nationally for week of July 13 due to renewed Hormuz fighting; LTL Midwest embargoes appearing from June volume backlogs
DAT week of July 14: dry van $3.05/mile (-1¢ WoW, +55% YoY, above contract rates since Feb 2022); flatbed $3.67/mile; reefer $3.43/mile; load posts +22% WoW to 3.34M
Citizens Bank initiated C.H. Robinson at Outperform $235 July 15; CHR's June 22 DeSpir Logistics acquisition ($75M) ended 5-year M&A drought; CEO says AI will drive consolidation
Section 301 tariffs T-7 days to July 24 implementation: no extension announced; JOC reports Q3 booking freeze; August imports forecast at record levels
USMCA Round 3 begins July 20 in Mexico City; open issues down from 54 to 14; Canada excluded; US chose annual review over 16-year renewal locking in cross-border uncertainty
NRF projects July U.S. container imports will hit all-time record 2.47M TEU from Section 301 front-loading, with August forecast to drop to 2.22M TEU (-4.5% YoY).
FTR/Truckstop: Flatbed spot rates posted second-largest weekly drop ever (-14.6 cents) week ending July 10; dry van down 4.5% WoW; rates still +36-45% YoY.
Uber Freight DocAI won FreightWaves 2026 AI Excellence Award (Operational AI Integration), processing 20,000+ PODs/week at 99%+ precision and cutting invoice disputes 50%.
PepsiCo disclosed 35 fully driverless Gatik trucks operating live in Arizona supply chain (Walmart/Dollar General) with zero accidents; 250-location expansion in AZ/TX/AR underway.
FMCSA activated Aurora warning device waiver July 10 allowing driverless trucks alternative roadside safety measures; Aurora Q2 results and Gen-2 observerless hardware update due July 29.
Section 301 tariffs implementing July 24 (T-4 days): 10-12.5% on 99.4% of U.S. imports by value; Brazil 25% effective July 22; no extension legislation pending.
FMCSA three deregulatory rules effective July 22: CDL self-reporting requirement removed, physical ELD manual rescinded, roadside inspection corrected-report procedures revised.
USMCA Round 3 begins July 21 in Mexico City: U.S. demands 82% automotive RVC + 50% US-specific content + Chinese component restrictions; open issues reduced from 54 to 14.
C.H. Robinson Q2 earnings July 29 (consensus $1.49 EPS, +15.5% YoY); CEO says AI drives brokerage consolidation; Lean AI Engineer claims 17-81% load reduction for 4PL clients.
RXO Q2 EBITDA guided $27-37M vs Q1 adjusted EBITDA loss; managed transportation $100M+ new freight awards with pipeline up $200M; agentic AI processed 500K+ carrier calls in Q1.
ArcBest brand consolidation effective August 1: MoLo Solutions and Panther fold into ArcBest; 280 jobs cut; 10 LTL service centers closing; $40M annualized savings targeted.
Echo Global Logistics launched full intra-Mexico transportation services integrated with EchoXBorder customs brokerage, expanding cross-border capabilities.
Blue Yonder (Gartner TMS MQ Leader, 19th year) and SAP both deploying persistent agentic AI execution layers targeting enterprise TMS buyers on top of existing stacks.
Logistics Viewpoints analysis: future TMS buyer purchasing bundled technology + execution capacity + market access + analytics, not software alone.

Trump signed three Section 338 proclamations July 21 imposing 50% additional tariffs on Canadian goods (autos, dairy, alcohol, wood products, paper, machinery, textiles) effective August 16 2026; USMCA-qualifying goods explicitly NOT exempt; covers ~$20B in Canadian imports; Canada PM Carney called it a direct USMCA violation
DAT week July 12-18 2026: dry van $2.99/mile (+55% YoY), reefer $3.43/mile (+45% YoY), flatbed $3.62/mile; load-to-truck ratios: van 12.1x, reefer 23.6x, flatbed 53.3x

Section 301 tariffs implementing July 24 (3 days): 10-12.5% on 99.4% of US imports from 60 countries, no time limit, no rate cap, no grandfathering; JOC warns Q3 booking freeze as shippers await compliance clarity

USMCA Round 3 underway July 21 in Mexico City: US demands 82% automotive RVC + 50% US-specific content + Chinese component restrictions; Canada simultaneously hit with 50% Section 338 tariffs making USMCA renewal more distant

Aurora Innovation Q2 2026 Business Review scheduled July 29: will confirm Gen-2 observerless hardware deployment (1M-mile lifespan 50%+ cost reduction), progress toward 200+ driverless trucks year-end; AUR stock $6.09 July 20; Uber Freight is named customer
RXO Q2 2026 earnings August 6: guided $27-37M adjusted EBITDA vs near-zero Q1; Managed Transportation won $100M+ new freight awards with pipeline up $200M; agentic AI processed 500K+ carrier calls Q1

C.H. Robinson Q2 earnings July 29: consensus $1.49 EPS (+15.5% YoY); 30 AI agents processing 100% of rate quotes in 32 seconds; CEO Bozeman says AI will drive brokerage consolidation and positions CHR as the winner

Flexport resurrected Convoy digital platform as Convoy Platform targeting small carriers and owner-operators, re-entering digital brokerage from tech/forwarding side alongside IPO readiness at $2.1B revenue +30% YoY

Carrier bankruptcy wave continues mid-July 2026: Daniel Trucking International (IL), Lynda Transportation (IL), TJ Trucking Enterprises (OH), Jackson and Son Hauling (VA), Victory Freight Corp (CA) all filed Chapter 7/11; Fusion Transport LLC (NJ) ending 79 workers; Ryder Integrated Logistics 76 permanent layoffs Indiana July 31
